stories.title: ‘God squad’ waives endangered species law to allow US drilling in Gulf of Mexico
stories.summary: Critics say exemption for fossil fuels exploits White House’s ‘self-made gas crisis’, and could doom the rare Rice’s whale Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A US government panel on Tuesday exempted oil and gas drilling in the Gulf of Mexico from the Endangered Species Act (ESA), a move which critics say could doom a rare whale species and harm other marine life . The Endangered Species Committee – which had not convened in more than three decades – vo

stories.title: Tusk and Irish PM call Hungarian foreign minister’s alleged links to Russia ‘repulsive’ and ‘sinister’ – Europe live
stories.summary: Donald Tusk and Micheál Martin say reported phone call with Moscow on sanctions confirms Hungary ‘doing the bidding for Russia’ within EU Back to Iran and the perceived lack of support from European Nato allies, US president Donald Trump has now turned to criticising France in his latest outburst on social media. In a post on Truth Social, he said: “The Country of France wouldn’t let planes headed to Israel, loaded up with military supplies, fly over French territory. France has been VERY UNHELP

stories.title: Removing EU flags is ‘betrayal,’ French minister tells far-right mayors
stories.summary: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot has condemned the removal of EU flags from town halls by far-right mayors, calling it a “betrayal.” Several newly elected mayors from the anti-immigration, EU-skeptic National Rally party have taken down EU flags from government buildings in recent days following last week’s municipal elections, in which the far right won 55 new municipal mandates. Barrot responded on X on Tuesday: “This is a betrayal of who we are. The European Union is something France

stories.title: Viktor Orbán has the support of both Russia and the US. Here’s why that doesn’t seem to be doing him any good | Péter Krekó
stories.summary: In Hungary, the Iran war is exposing tensions. On April 12, voters may decide that Orban’s geopolitical contortions are a liability On 3 March, Viktor Orbán held a phone conversation with Vladimir Putin. According to official Hungarian reporting, the discussion focused on “energy issues” and other routine matters. What followed was anything but routine. Within days, the Hungarian foreign minister, Péter Szijjártó , had flown to Moscow, and returned with two freed prisoners of war , dual citizens

stories.title: Can Europe’s public service media survive attacks by the far right?
stories.summary: From Italy to France, Germany to Hungary, far-right governments and politicians are targeting media with the same playbook Revealed: Five EU governments found to ‘consistently’ dismantle rule of law Barely six months after Giorgia Meloni’s government was sworn in, the chief executive of Italy’s public broadcaster Rai resigned. Carlo Fuortes cited “a political conflict” as the reason for his departure in May 2023, a year before the end of his term. The top posts quickly went to nominees with ties

stories.title: Wolf bites woman in Hamburg shopping area in Germany's first attack in decades
stories.summary: Wolves are now present in almost all EU countries, and their numbers have increased from 11,000 in 2012 to more than 20,000 in 2023. Italy, Bulgaria, Romania and Spain have populations of more than 2,000.

stories.title: Orbán's campaign rallies highlight deep divisions among Hungarian voters
stories.summary: At Orbán’s rallies, standoffs between protesters and organized teams working to silence them have produced scenes charged with extreme tension in the run-up to the Hungarian elections.

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
stories.summary: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: Vanuatu Indigenous leaders raise concerns over plans to build resort for cruise tourists
stories.summary: Exclusive: Environmental impact assessments are ‘incomplete’, say leaders, and private beach club could harm fragile ecosystems Indigenous community leaders in Vanuatu have raised concerns over plans by the cruise operator Royal Caribbean to build a private beach club on the island of Lelepa, arguing environmental impact assessments by the company are “incomplete” and “misleading”. The community leaders outlined the issues in a letter sent to Royal Caribbean on 26 February, which has been seen b

stories.title: Körsétány, nagy parkok, megfizethető lakások – ezekből a tervekből állhat össze a rákosrendezői mesterterv
stories.summary: A Főváros tervpályázatán 14 pályaműből választották ki azt a négyet, amikből 2028-ra összeállhat a nagy terv. Karácsony Gergely szerint ez történelmi nap a város életében, Vitézy Dávid szerint Budapest rég mert ekkora ambíciókat dédelgetni.

stories.title: New audio recording reveals Hungarian FM promising Lavrov assistance on sanctions
stories.summary: A group of investigative journalists have obtained and verified the transcripts and the recordings of Péter Szijjártó's conversations with Sergey Lavrov and another high-ranking Russian official.

stories.title: Izraeli–magyar védelmi ipari központot nyit Budapesten egy ismert izraeli ingatlanbefektető
stories.summary: Arie Yom-Tov szerint Izraelnek Budapest biztonságos hely, magyarországi gyártási lehetőségekkel és egy egész Európát kiszolgáló budapesti bemutatóteremmel invitálja ide az izraeli védelmi szektort.

stories.title: Megszólalt a budapesti gimnázium igazgatója, aki nem engedi levetíteni Molnár Áronék új filmjét az iskolai filmklubban
stories.summary: Tömpe László a Telexnek azt írta, döntését emberi és szakmai szempontok vezérlik, amelyeket mindig a gyerekek érdekében hoz meg.

stories.title: A FIFA-elnök szerint nincs B meg C tervük, Iránnak ott kell lennie a foci-vb-n
stories.summary: Gianni Infantino szerint biztosítani akarják, hogy az iráni csapat a lehető legjobb körülmények között vehessen részt a világbajnokságon.

stories.title: Dobrev Klára szerint nem kétséges, hogy a DK stabil parlamenti párt
stories.summary: Nem számít további DK-s jelöltek visszalépésére, az pedig fel sem merült, hogy a párt ne induljon el a választáson. A Telex élő műsorában amellett érvelt, hogy szükség van a DK-ra a kormányváltás érdekében.

stories.title: Ördögien megrendezett menet a halálba – így tűnt el Pécs zsidó közössége
stories.summary: A helyi értelmiség és a kereskedelem fontos tagjai voltak a pécsi zsidók. Büszke magyarok voltak, akiket magyarok semmiztek ki, tartottak embertelen körülmények között, majd küldték a halálba.

stories.title: Az ukrán külügyminiszter szerint Magyarország túszul ejtette az Európai Uniót
stories.summary: Andrij Szibiha a bucsai mészárlás negyedik évfordulóján beszélt erről.

stories.title: „Csak nem nyer még egyszer a Fidesz” vs. „a Tisza sem lesz jobb” – elsőszavazók az első választásukról
stories.summary: Az áprilisi választáson 181 ezer olyan fiatal szavaz életében először, akik 2-4 éves koruk óta az Orbán-kormányok alatt élnek. Közülük többen úgy gondolják, most van esély a változásra, és végre lezárulhat a korábbi „nyuggerpolitizálás”, de vannak olyanok is, akik óvatosak a Tisza Párttal. Egy februári felmérés szerint megalázó a Fidesz hátránya a 18–29-es korosztályban.

stories.title: Fact-check: Tényleg halált okozhat az egyik legnépszerűbb édesítőszer?
stories.summary: Az elmúlt napokban megint nagyot ment a magyar interneten egy tavalyi tanulmány, amely szerint az eritrit károsíthatja az agyi ereket.

stories.title: A szülők 80 százaléka bűntudatot érez, amikor visszatér a munkába
stories.summary: Sok munkáltató úgy tekint a kisgyerekkel otthon töltött időre, mint egy lyukra az önéletrajzban, és irreális, ha a munka és a magánélet egyensúlyára akarunk törekedni. Interjú Szijjártó Linda munka- és szervezetpszichológussal a Holnap legújabb adásában.

stories.title: Blikk: Holtan találtak egy magyar kamionost Ausztriában
stories.summary: Még nem tudják, mi okozta a hatvanéves férfi halálát.

stories.title: Már 4 dollár felett jár egy gallon benzin az USA-ban, és emiatt Donald Trump aggódhat a legjobban
stories.summary: Az iráni konfliktus miatt az ukrajnai háború kirobbanása óta nem látott szintre ment fel az üzemanyagok ára, amit az amerikai választók nem szoktak értékelni.

stories.title: A lengyelek adócsökkentéssel mérséklik a benzinárat, de Trump közbeszólhat
stories.summary: Lengyelországban naponta határozzák meg a benzinkúti árakat, amelyekre így a legnagyobb hatással jelen állás szerint Donald Trump lehet.

stories.title: Tusk: Orbán és külügyminisztere már régen elhagyták Európát
stories.summary: A lengyel miniszterelnök is megnyilvánult a Szijjártó és Lavrov közötti telefonbeszélgetések keddi nyilvánosságra kerülése után.

stories.title: „Szia, Gundalf, Magyar Péter. Na hogy vagy? Élsz?” – videón, ahogy Magyar felhívja Gundalfot
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt vezetője támogatásáról is biztosította a hétfőn nyilatkozó informatikust.

stories.title: Az a baj, hogy akkor tartjuk jónak a demokráciát, ha a mi emberünk van hatalmon
stories.summary: Van egy nagy elégedetlenség a világban amiatt, ahogy működnek a demokráciák, de hogyan lehetne megjavítani? Miért hasznos, ha van egy kis megosztottság? Miben segítene, ha már a 16 évesek is szavazhatnának? Magyarország jövő időben, közönség előtt.

stories.title: „Az én taktikám nem a békepipa, hanem a tomahawk” – kijött az előzetes Orbán Viktor és Hajdú Péter összeköltözéséhez
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a G-napról és a Matolcsy-klánról is társalog a műsorvezetővel felcsúti házában.

stories.title: Hónapokon belül eldőlhet, ki és milyen moduláris reaktort építhet Magyarországon
stories.summary: Nem akarunk lemaradni, a szabályozás már elkészült, piaci alapon működő SMR viszont még nincs. A technológiában már biztosak vagyunk, csak azt nem tudjuk, melyik céget válasszuk.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a Lavrovval egyeztető Szijjártóról beszélt, majd a Btk.-ból emlegette a hazaárulást
stories.summary: Magyar országjárásának korábbi, kabai állomásán Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz is megszólalt.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor Ócsán: Jó volna tudni, hogy vannak-e itt tiszások
stories.summary: Nem nagyon voltak. Fölösleges kockázat és balhé nélkül zajlott a miniszterelnök keddi fellépése.

stories.title: Szijjártó: Nem kért tőlem semmit semmilyen oligarcha
stories.summary: A külügyminiszter szerint nem csak az oroszoktól kap tippeket, hogy mivel érveljen egyes szankcionált emberek vagy szervezetek mentesítéséért.

stories.title: A MÁV vezérigazgatója szerint nincs a 10 legnagyobb problémájuk között, hogy új székházba költözzenek
stories.summary: Hegyi Zsolt azt mondja: még nem látott olyan költözést, amit ne lehetett volna visszacsinálni.

stories.title: Letörölték a Euronews magyar felületéről a NER-cégek tőzsdei mélyrepüléséről szóló cikket
stories.summary: Kiszivárgott egy belsős levél az anyagot jegyző újságírótól. A cég függetlenségéért sokan aggódnak azóta, hogy egy Orbán-rendszerhez köthető szereplő felvásárolta évekkel ezelőtt.

stories.title: Hullámzó játékkal ért el gól nélküli döntetlent a görögök ellen a fociválogatott
stories.summary: Második idei mérkőzésén nem tudott győzni a magyar válogatott, gól nélküli döntetlent ért el a Puskás Arénában a görögökkel szemben.

stories.title: „Ők is tudják, hogy a következő négy évben Tisza-kormány lesz” – egy teljes napot töltöttünk Magyar Péterrel
stories.summary: Elkísértük Magyar Pétert hat kampányhelyszínre szombaton. Végig a Tisza elnökével utaztunk a párt autójában, és ott voltunk, amikor értesül az Alkotmányvédelmi Hivatal videójáról.

stories.title: A cseh államfő szerint országának újra kellene értékelnie kapcsolatát Magyarországgal
stories.summary: Pert Pavel szerint elfogadhatatlan, hogy egy EU- és NATO-tagállam érzékeny információkat oszt meg Moszkvával.

stories.title: US reopens embassy in Venezuela in significant thawing of relations
stories.summary: Resumption of diplomatic operations come three months after former president Maduro was abducted Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US government is resuming operations at its embassy in Venezuela, the state department announced on Monday, nearly three months since former president Nicolás Maduro was abducted from the country and locked up in the US. The resumption of US diplomatic operations in Venezuela marks a significant step in the US-Venezuela

stories.title: Avi Lewis, elected to lead Canada’s New Democratic party, promises ‘NDP comeback’
stories.summary: Ex-TV host pledged to centre party around equity, with higher wealth taxes, green energy and tuition-free education Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Canada’s embattled New Democratic party (NDP) has elected the former broadcaster and self-proclaimed socialist Avi Lewis as its new leader, as it looks to rebuild following a devastating federal election last year that saw it lose official party status. A record number of members voted in the three-day ND

stories.title: In an Istanbul market, I found an old German phrase book – and a reminder of how not to speak to migrants | Carolin Würfel
stories.summary: Turkish immigrants to Germany in the 60s were seen as temporary labour, not people. Today’s government in Berlin is at risk of repeating the mistake A few weekends ago, I went to the flea market in Bomonti, a neighbourhood on the European side of Istanbul. I go there regularly, and over the years I’ve accumulated a small collection of things: embroidered napkins, records, old issues of House & Garden, earrings, candle holders. It is usually on the days when you are not looking for anything i

stories.title: US supreme court appears skeptical of jury process in racial bias death row case
stories.summary: Case involves a former prosecutor removing nearly all Black jurors in a 2006 capital murder trial, raising legal questions The US supreme court appeared skeptical on Tuesday of whether jury selection in a trial was conducted appropriately when they heard oral arguments in a death penalty case about racial bias in jury selection stemming from Mississippi. Doug Evans, a now-retired prosecutor, removed all but one Black person from a jury that convicted Terry Pitchford of capital murder in 2006. Th

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: US has begun flying B52 bombers over Iran, Pentagon says
stories.summary: Top US general Dan Caine says US has growing air superiority over Iran; Trump tells allies ‘go get your own oil’ in latest angry outburst Saudi Arabia’s defence ministry has said it has intercepted and destroyed ten drones over the past hours, and eight missiles launched towards the Riyadh area and the country’s eastern region. Early this morning Kuwait said its air defences were responding to hostile missile and drone attacks. Neither Saudi Arabia nor Kuwait said where the drones or missiles ca

stories.title: Iran threatens to strike US-owned tech companies in the Middle East
stories.summary: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps threatened Tuesday to target American tech and defense companies operating in the Middle East if the U.S. and Israel continue to target Iranian leadership. In a statement  released by the government-backed Tasnim News Agency, the IRGC said retaliatory attacks could begin as early as Wednesday evening and advised employees and residents living within a 1 kilometer radius of the U.S.-owned facilities to evacuate the area. The IRGC listed 18 companies it t

stories.title: Italy denies use of Sicily airbase to US planes carrying weapons for Iran war
stories.summary: Defence ministry says US failed to request authorisation in time for parliament to give approval as required by international treaty Italy has denied the use of an airbase in Sicily to US military planes carrying weapons for the war in Iran after the US did not follow the required authorisation procedure. A source at the Italian defence ministry confirmed a report in Corriere della Sera that “some US bombers” had been due to land at Sigonella – one of seven US navy bases in Italy – before headin

stories.title: ‘There’s no safe place here’: Kuwaiti tanker hit by Iranian drone attack in Dubai port
stories.summary: Dozens of other vessels leave area after drone strike causes fire onboard tanker owned by Kuwait’s state oil company Middle East crisis – live updates How could US forcibly reopen strait of Hormuz and what are the risks? When Iran attacked a fully loaded crude oil tanker anchored at Dubai port on Monday night, damaging the vessel’s hull, hundreds of seafarers stranded on tankers anchored nearby were close enough to watch as the vessel burned. Thousands more were able to listen to radio messages

stories.title: Israel vows to occupy swathes of southern Lebanon to expand buffer zone
stories.summary: Defence minister outlines plans to destroy all homes and villages in area ‘in accordance with the model in Gaza’ Middle East crisis live – latest updates Israel says it will occupy swathes of south Lebanon and destroy the homes along the border to prevent the return of about 600,000 residents, prompting concerns of long-term forced displacement. The defence minister, Israel Katz, said that when fighting with Hezbollah ended, Israel would occupy the area under the Litani River, about 19 miles fro

stories.title: King’s state visit to US will take place in April despite calls to delay amid Iran war – UK politics live
stories.summary: The king will address Congress during the visit, which will commemorate the 250th anniversary of American independence Q: What do you make of the suggestion that Donald Trump could end the Iran war without securing the strait of Hormuz? Or do you think he should finish the job? Farage replies: I don’t think we should take literally anything right now that Donald Trump says … And then the last thing he’s going to do, or the last thing his colleagues in the White House are going to do, is to give

stories.title: ‘Discriminatory’ Israeli death penalty law would be war crime, says UN rights chief
stories.summary: Volker Türk says bill, which applies to Palestinians convicted of terror charges but not Jewish extremists, must be repealed A new Israeli law that would allow the execution of Palestinians convicted on terror charges for deadly attacks, but not Jewish extremists accused of similar crimes, would constitute a war crime if enacted, according to one of the UN’s most senior human rights officials. Speaking amid mounting international condemnation of the bill, the UN’s high commissioner for human rig

stories.title: TMZ tracks US lawmakers vacationing amid the partial government shutdown
stories.summary: Tabloid outlet has covered Republicans and Democrats relaxing at places such as Disney World as shutdown drags on When US federal workers were missing paychecks and the partial government shutdown entered its seventh week, Lindsey Graham, a Republican senator from South Carolina, was doing what any responsible lawmaker would do: riding Space Mountain and carrying a bubble wand at Disney World in Florida. Naturally, TMZ had photos of the vacationing senator on its homepage a few days later. Conti

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
stories.summary: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.

stories.title: Emergency services respond to fire in central Israel after Iranian missile attack
stories.summary: Emergency teams responded to a fire in central Israel after Iranian missile strikes, with several people reported injured.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.summary: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Italy blocks US use of Sicily air base for Middle East war
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Italy has refused permission for U.S. military aircraft to land at the Sigonella ​air base in Sicily before flying to ‌the Middle East, an Italian government official confirmed on Tuesday. Some U.S. bombers had planned to land in Sigonella last week before flying onward, but their flight plan was not communicated in advance to the Italian air force general staff, nor had the American aircraft received authorization to land, according to Corriere della Sera . As these were not logistic

stories.title: French far right goes after Trump, Big Oil amid spiraling energy crisis
stories.summary: PARIS — The National Rally is hitting out at U.S. President Donald Trump, the French government and large oil companies for variously causing and profiting from soaring energy prices due to the war in the Middle East. The far-right party has pushed for lower taxes on gas and urgent market controls to shield consumers from the spiraling crisis, doubling down on its cost-of-living platform that has made it the front-runner for next year’s presidential election under Marine Le Pen’s lea

stories.title: Pakistan and China propose five-part peace plan for Middle East
stories.summary: Foreign ministers Ishaq Dar and Wang Yi met in Beijing as Pakistan pushes for peacemaker role Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan and China have released a joint five-part proposal for peace in the Middle East, after Pakistan’s foreign minister flew to Beijing on Tuesday to seek Chinese support for the country’s faltering efforts to negotiate an end to end the war. The one-day meeting between Ishaq Dar and his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, came as Pakistan continues to push for the role o

stories.title: UK sends more troops to Gulf amid Trump jibes over British military role
stories.summary: John Healey says extra deployment is defensive response to ‘expanding threat’ from Iran Middle East crisis – live updates UK politics live – latest updates The UK is sending more military support to the Gulf, taking the total deployment to 1,000 troops, amid more jibes from Donald Trump about Britain’s refusal to get involved in offensive operations against Iran. Speaking from Qatar where he met UK troops, the defence secretary, John Healey, said the extra deployment was in response to an “expan

stories.title: ‘Get your own oil’: Trump launches tirade against Europe for not joining Iran war
stories.summary: Many countries in Europe have called the conflict illegal, with some blocking Israeli and US planes from moving weapons through their airspace Donald Trump has launched a tirade against European countries that refused to join his war against Iran, calling out the UK and France, as transatlantic relations soured from the spiralling conflict that has wreaked havoc on the global economy. On his Truth Social website, the US president told governments worried about fuel prices to “go get your own oil

stories.title: Almost half of primary teachers in England see pupils with eating disorders, survey finds
stories.summary: Poll of 10,000 teachers also finds ‘overwhelming’ exam anxiety and rising absenteeism linked to poor mental health Almost half of primary school teachers are seeing pupils with eating disorders “at least occasionally”, rising to four in five at secondary level, according to a survey by the UK’s largest education union. The findings emerged in a poll of 10,000 teachers in English state schools about pupils’ mental health, which also revealed “overwhelming” exam anxiety in secondaries and dwindlin

stories.title: Merz criticised after calling for Syrians in Germany to ‘go back’ home
stories.summary: The German chancellor has drawn condemnation from NGOs and members of his own government Friedrich Merz has drawn condemnation from NGOs and members of his own government after he called for the vast majority of Syrians living in Germany to “go back to their homeland”. The German chancellor, who was elected last year after promising a tough line on immigration in a bid to beat the far right, made the remarks during a visit to Berlin on Monday by the interim Syrian president Ahmed al-Sharaa . Con

stories.title: Nigel Farage’s biggest problem? Donald Trump
stories.summary: Nearly a quarter of voters cite Reform leader’s support for US president as main reason against voting for his party By day 31 of the war in the Middle East, Nigel Farage had become somewhat less vocal about the closeness of his relationship with Donald Trump. “Trying to read what’s really in the minds of people in the White House right at the moment is a mug’s game,” said the MP, as he unveiled his party’s latest “pledge” to cut the cost of living on Tuesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK to pay France extra £16m in stopgap deal to patrol Channel beaches
stories.summary: Two-month arrangement aimed at preventing small-boat crossings comes as existing deal expires The UK will pay France an extra £16.2m to keep police patrolling Channel beaches and prevent a surge in small-boat crossings after negotiators failed to agree a permanent deal before a midnight deadline. The stopgap arrangement, which will last for two months, comes after French negotiators refused to agree to UK demands for further interventions and patrols to stop asylum seekers from reaching the UK v

stories.title: Supreme court rules against Colorado ban on ‘conversion therapy’ in decision rebuked by one justice – US politics live
stories.summary: In lone dissent, Ketanji Brown Jackson says majority ‘has failed to appreciate crucial context’ of constitutional claims in the case Sign up for the Breaking News US email Donald Trump confirmed that King Charles and Queen Camilla, will travel to the US for a state visit from 27 to 30 April . The president said that the trip will include a banquet dinner at the White House on 28 April. “I look forward to spending time with the King, whom I greatly respect. It will be TERRIFIC!,” Trump wrote on T

stories.title: Zimbabwe split as public weighs electoral reforms bill
stories.summary: Zimbabwe has begun nationwide public hearings on a proposed bill that could extend President Mnangagwa's term and overhaul the electoral system. Supporters call it stabilizing, but critics warn it threatens democracy.

stories.title: Local elections in Serbia: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?
stories.summary: Serbia's ruling SNS once again secured victories in local elections — but sometimes just by a few hundred votes. Narrowing margins suggest a shifting political landscape and growing momentum for anti-government protests.

stories.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba, the 1st since US blockade
stories.summary: The tanker carrying some 730,000 barrels of crude oil is sanctioned by the US, the EU and the UK amid the war in Ukraine. It comes as Cuba struggles with severe shortages after Trump's total oil blockade since January.

stories.title: Iran war: EU calls Tehran to discuss Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: European Council President Antonio Costa has urged Tehran to engage diplomatically on the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, US President Donald Trump told NATO allies to “go get your own oil” amid shortages. DW has more.

stories.title: Iran launches missile and drone barrage on Dubai, Kuwaiti tanker hit
stories.summary: At least five explosions were reported in Dubai on Tuesday morning as residents fled to shelters, Euronews journalists reported. The UAE authorities said they were "actively engaging" with the threat.

stories.title: Watch: EU governments are throwing billions at the petrol shock. Will it help?
stories.summary: European drivers face soaring fuel prices as the Iran war hits oil markets, prompting EU states to cut fuel taxes, cap prices and tighten regulation.

stories.title: Lebanese justice minister calls for Israel negotiations to avert humanitarian catastrophe
stories.summary: Lebanese justice minister warns of 'dramatic' situation in the country as Israeli floats extended buffer zone on its southern border. Nassar calls for diplomacy to avert a catastrophe as he lashes at Hezbolahh for sabotaging diplomatic efforts by not laying down weapons.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.summary: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: Moment US strike sends massive fireball into sky over Iran’s Isfahan
stories.summary: Large explosions and fires were seen over Isfahan, a central Iranian city hosting military and nuclear facilities.

stories.title: Israeli army clashes with Palestinians during north Jerusalem raid
stories.summary: Israeli army clashes with Palestinians during an overnight raid in Kafr Aqab north of Jerusalem

stories.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba after US allows passage despite energy blockade
stories.summary: Cuba used to receive most of its oil from Venezuela, but those shipments were halted ever since the US attacked the South American country and arrested its leader Nicolás Maduro in early January.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Iran war goals achieved 'beyond halfway point' as strikes continue across Middle East
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, Trump's partner in attacking Iran, said more than half of his military aims had been achieved, but both leaders refused to put a timeline on an operation that has ignited a month-long regional war and jolted global markets.

stories.title: EU warnt vor „sehr ernster Lage“: Europäer sollen von zu Hause arbeiten
stories.summary: BRÜSSEL – Die EU-Kommission hat die Europäer am Dienstag dazu aufgefordert, von zu Hause zu arbeiten, weniger Auto zu fahren und weniger zu fliegen. Gleichzeitig ermahnte sie die Mitgliedsländer, in der während des Iran-Kriegs anhaltenden Energiekrise dringend die erneuerbaren Energien auszubauen. In einer Rede, die an die Anfänge der Coronavirus-Pandemie erinnerte, warnte der EU-Energiechef Dan Jørgensen vor einer „sehr ernsten Lage“, deren Ende nicht absehbar sei. „Selbst wenn morgen Frieden h

stories.title: Irish lawmaker urges Stripe to flout US sanctions on UN investigator Albanese
stories.summary: An Irish lawmaker in the European Parliament has urged the Irish brothers who own payments giant Stripe to allow payments outside the U.S. for United Nations official Francesca Albanese. The pressure on Stripe comes after the U.S. placed Albanese, the U.N.’s special rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Palestinian territories, under sanctions . Liberal lawmaker Barry Andrews, from the Renew group, appealed to Stripe co-founders John and Patrick Collison to “facilitate b

stories.title: US-based dissident artist put on trial in China over satirical Mao sculptures, says rights group
stories.summary: New York-based Gao Zhen was detained in 2024 during a family visit to China and then tried for ‘defaming national heroes’ The Chinese dissident artist Gao Zhen , known for making satirical sculptures of China’s former leader Mao Zedong, has been tried over accusations of “defaming national heroes and martyrs”, his wife and a rights group have said. Gao, 69, who was detained in 2024 during a visit to China from the US, faces a maximum three-year prison sentence, his wife, Zhao Yaliang, and Shane

stories.title: Russia slowly trying to splinter its internet from rest of world, analysts say
stories.summary: Telegram is increasingly blocked and mobile internet users face blackouts in effort likened to Iranian shutdowns Russia is in the midst of a vast, slow-moving effort to splinter its internet from the rest of the world, say activists and experts, with steep consequences for millions of people who are gradually being cut off. Unlike Iran’s internet shutdowns earlier this year, Russia’s shutdown is a piecemeal and opaque effort. It is defined by escalating mobile internet blackouts across cities an

stories.title: Landlords ‘leveraging up’ by exploiting property tax rules are fuelling Australia’s housing affordability crisis, analysis finds
stories.summary: Exclusive: Capital gains tax discount and negative gearing rules created ‘extra artificial incentive’ for property speculation, the e61 Institute has found Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The combination of the capital gains tax discount and negative gearing rules has turbocharged debt-fuelled property speculation over recent decades, according to a new analysis of hundreds of thousands of property investments. The federal budget in three weeks’ time is widely expect

stories.title: Deepwater discoveries: scientists find more than 110 new fish and invertebrate species in the Coral Sea
stories.summary: Brittlestars, sea anemones and a catshark among new-to-science species collected during expedition off the Queensland coast Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Marine scientists have discovered more than 110 new fish and invertebrate species in the Coral Sea – a figure they believe could exceed 200 as more are identified. The species were found in waters between 200 metres and 3km deep in the Coral Sea marine park, Australia’s largest marine protected area, which spans n

stories.title: Labor’s draft party platform more assertive on China and omits mandatory jail term stance
stories.summary: Exclusive: the 2026 document is designed to provide ‘scaffolding’ for a long-term Labor government Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor’s longstanding opposition to mandatory jail terms has been omitted from the first draft of its new national party platform, after the Albanese government backed minimum sentences on several occasions. An early working draft of the ALP’s updated platform also includes more assertive language on China and seeks to position Australia a

stories.title: Penguin to sue OpenAI over ChatGPT version of German children’s book
stories.summary: Publisher alleges AI research company’s chatbot violated its copyright over Coconut the Little Dragon series Penguin Random House has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging its chatbot ChatGPT violated copyright by mimicking and reproducing the content of a popular series of German children’s books. The lawsuit, which was filed on Friday with a Munich court against OpenAI’s Ireland-based European subsidiary, states Penguin Random House’s legal team had prompted ChatGPT to write a story in the

stories.title: Human remains found on California beach in 2022 identified as missing man
stories.summary: DNA testing confirmed bones found on Salmon Creek beach belonged to Walter Karl Kinney, a man last seen in 1999 Human remains discovered in the summer of 2022 on a beach in California recently were identified as those of a former banker who disappeared in 1999. The DNA Doe Project (DDP) on Thursday announced that bones found on Salmon Creek state beach in northern California in June 2022 – by a family searching for seashells – belonged to 59-year-old Walter Karl Kinney, a former banker who lived

stories.title: Marmite maker Unilever agrees $44.8bn deal to combine food arm with McCormick
stories.summary: Core focus will be on beauty, personal care and home products after spinning off brands such as Hellmann’s and Pot Noodle Unilever has agreed to combine its food business with US-based McCormick in a $44.8bn deal that will give the Marmite-to-Hellmann’s mayonnaise owner majority control of a food empire. The Anglo-Dutch company will control 65% of the new spin-off, which will combine brands such as Knorr and Pot Noodle with McCormick’s condiments and spices including French’s mustard, Old Bay se

stories.title: Head of NHS England ‘really worried’ about medicine supplies
stories.summary: Jim Mackey responds to concerns about cost implications and supply disruption link to war against Iran Middle East crisis – live updates The head of the NHS in England has said he is “really worried” about medicine supply issues. A number of experts have raised concerns about cost implications and supply disruption linked to the war in Iran . Continue reading...

stories.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
stories.summary: Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said. Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
stories.summary: Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods. Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February. The new mandate says schools m

stories.title: Meta, Tiktok and Google under investigation for allegedly disobeying Australia’s social media ban
stories.summary: Nearly 70% of under-16s with accounts on Instagram, Snapchat or TikTok had maintained access, survey finds The Australian government has accused big tech firms like Meta, TikTok and Google of disobeying the landmark ban on under-16s using social media, after the country’s online safety office warned many children had accounts. A survey of 900 Australian parents found around a third (31%) said their children still had one or more social media accounts after the ban, compared to 49% before the law

stories.title: Missile debris ignites pesticide plant fire in southern Israel
stories.summary: Heavy black smoke rose over an Israeli industrial plant after intercepted Iranian missile debris hit a pesticide tank, as Israel and Iran trade cross-border strikes.

stories.title: Ukraine: Top EU diplomats mark 4 years since Bucha massacre
stories.summary: Brussels' chief diplomat and several EU foreign ministers are in Ukraine to mark four years since one of Russia's most infamous atrocities. The Bucha massacre, just weeks into the full-scale war, shocked the world.

stories.title: Germany: Wolf bites woman in Hamburg
stories.summary: It is the first recorded wolf attack on a human since wolves returned to Germany nearly 30 years ago. The wolf likely wandered into the city by accident.

stories.title: Iran war: Chinese propaganda casts US as reckless aggressor
stories.summary: Chinese state media is subtly framing the US as a belligerent, destabilizing force in the Middle East. One AI-generated video features a Persian cat and an eagle locked in battle, symbolizing the Iran war.

stories.title: Has Pakistan outplayed India in Iran war diplomacy?
stories.summary: Pakistan is gaining diplomatic visibility during the Iran conflict, while questions are growing over India's quieter strategy.

stories.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
stories.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

stories.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
stories.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

stories.title: US Supreme Court rejects Colorado's 'conversion therapy' ban
stories.summary: Colorado had banned therapy seeking to change the sexual orientation or gender identity of LGBTQ patients under 18. Over 20 US states and much of Europe, including Germany, have banned the practice.

stories.title: Senegal: President signs off on LGBTQ law doubling maximum jail term for same-sex relations
stories.summary: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye enacted the new law after it resoundingly cleared parliament earlier this month. It also penalizes those "promoting" or funding same-sex relations, as well as false allegations.

stories.title: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
stories.summary: Victims describe how they have been targeted while carrying out everyday activities.

stories.title: UN Security Council to hold emergency session after peacekeeper deaths in Lebanon
stories.summary: The UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) said two of its personnel were killed Monday in an explosion and another died late Sunday when a projectile hit their position. The UN force said it launched an investigation into the separate incidents.

stories.title: Eurovision Song Contest launches first-ever Asia edition
stories.summary: Broadcasters from 10 countries, including South Korea and the Philippines, will be taking part.

stories.title: Attack on Michigan synagogue inspired by Iran-backed Hezbollah, FBI says
stories.summary: The FBI says the synagogue attack earlier in March was inspired by Hezbollah, targeting Michigan’s largest Jewish temple with a truck packed with fuel.

stories.title: Teen cyclist killed at Road World Championships not found for 82 minutes
stories.summary: A cyclist who was killed during the Road World Championships was not found for 82 minutes after a crash, investigators find.

stories.title: China bans storing cremated remains in empty 'bone ash apartments'
stories.summary: China bans 'bone ash apartments' where mourners are storing cremated remains instead of cemeteries.

stories.title: Eiffel Tower lights up in Paris for Celine Dion's return to the stage
stories.summary: To celebrate Celine Dion's 58th birthday and announce her official return to the stage, messages in several languages are projected onto the Eiffel Tower.

stories.title: Kissing a woman's hand can be sexual assault, Spanish court finds
stories.summary: The Supreme Court found that a man had done more than harass a woman by kissing her hand as there had been "a clear sexual component" to his behaviour.

stories.title: At least 16 killed and thousands displaced by gang attack in rural Haiti
stories.summary: The attack happened in the Artibonite region, where members of the Gran Grif gang have targeted locals in the past.

stories.title: Trump tells allies to ‘go get your own oil’ amid Iranian attacks on Gulf
stories.summary: Trump's comments come on the same day that gas prices in the US jumped past an average of $4 for the first time since 2022 as fuel prices continue to spike worldwide.

stories.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba ending near-total blockade
stories.summary: A Russian oil tanker has docked in Cuba, providing temporary relief after months of shortages.

stories.title: Wolf bites woman in shock German attack in Hamburg shopping street
stories.summary: It is believed to be the first wolf attack on a human since the animals began to reestablish themselves in Germany decades ago.

stories.title: With Spielberg's help, a 101-year-old Auschwitz survivor becomes warrior against hate
stories.summary: People who watch the countless interviews Ginette Kolinka gives cannot say that they didn’t know about the extermination of 6 million European Jews by the Nazis and their collaborators.

stories.title: Around 30% of France's teachers strike in protest against job cuts and low wages
stories.summary: The strike action comes as anger grows among France's teachers about job cuts, class closures and stagnating wages.

stories.title: This German village relies on renewables to avoid rising energy costs
stories.summary: The German village of Feldheim is using wind, solar and biogas energy to shield itself from rising energy prices linked to the Middle East conflict, with local officials saying residents are not affected by electricity cost increases.

stories.title: Police bust 'maze-like' drug tunnel to Morocco in Spanish exclave of Ceuta
stories.summary: Spain's close ties with Latin America and proximity to Morocco, a top cannabis producer, make it a key entry point for drugs into Europe.

stories.title: Slovenian women keep centuries-old ‘drsanke’ Easter egg art alive
stories.summary: In southern Slovenia, women revive ‘drsanke’, carving intricate Easter eggs in Bela Krajina, turning simple shells into lasting handmade gifts.

stories.title: King's state visit to US to go ahead in April, but no Harry meeting
stories.summary: Despite political tensions between the US and UK, the King will travel to Washington next month.

stories.title: Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law
stories.summary: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

stories.title: Syrian president discusses Iran war with British PM on first visit to London since fall of al-Assad
stories.summary: Ahmed al-Sharaa has managed to build relations with Western governments and made several overseas trips, including to the United States, France and Germany.

stories.title: Fire at chemical plant in Russia’s Nizhnekamsk leaves three dead
stories.summary: Footage shows thick smoke rising over an industrial site in Nizhnekamsk, western Russia.

stories.title: Iran has 'necessary will' to end war but seeking guarantees, Pezeshkian tells EU
stories.summary: US Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth said that the next days of the Iran war will be "decisive" while refusing to rule out US ground forces playing a role in the conflict.

stories.title: Palm Sunday bird sellers in Mexico City cling to fading tradition
stories.summary: Palm Sunday in Mexico City still sees pajareros lining the streets with birds and flowers, but tighter laws and criticism are shrinking the tradition.

stories.title: US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad and security forces pursuing captors, Iraqi officials say
stories.summary: It was not immediately clear if the kidnapping was related to the ongoing war, but Iran-backed militias in Iraq have launched regular attacks on US facilities in the country.

stories.title: Strikes hit southern Lebanese village, damage buildings and vehicles

stories.title: Israel approves death penalty for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks
stories.summary: The bill would make the death penalty the default punishment for Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank found guilty of intentionally carrying out deadly attacks deemed "acts of terrorism" by an Israeli military court.

stories.title: Paris ring road go-slow: French hauliers demand fuel aid from government
stories.summary: Hauliers from OTRE stage a go-slow protest on the Paris ring road, demanding stronger government support as soaring fuel prices squeeze margins.

stories.title: China to ban storing remains of dead in ‘bone ash apartments’
stories.summary: Practice of using apartments to store relatives’ ashes has risen as rapid urbanisation and ageing population increases competition for cemetery plots China is introducing a law to stop people storing the ashes of their dead relatives in empty high-rise flats rather than paying steep costs for increasingly scarce cemetery plots. China’s new funeral management legislation will prohibit the use of “residential housing specifically for the purpose of storing cremated remains” and the burial of corps

stories.title: Spheres of influence: the Bauhaus’s radical female photographers – in pictures
stories.summary: The images are famous, but the women who took them are often forgotten. An inspiring exhibition focuses on the pioneering ‘new vision’ of Marianne Brandt, Lucia Moholy and more Continue reading...

stories.title: Melbourne girl, 13, allegedly shouted antisemitic remarks, threw eggs and swerved car towards Jewish families
stories.summary: Jewish families ‘narrowly escaped being struck by the car’ after girl swerved towards them in Ripponlea, police say Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A 13-year-old Melbourne girl has been charged with 52 offences including allegedly shouting antisemitic remarks, throwing eggs and swerving a stolen car towards members of the Jewish community. Victorian police said in a statement on Tuesday that the girl and two othe

stories.title: Haiti: At least 70 killed in 'massacre,' says rights group
stories.summary: The death toll from a brutal gang attack in Haiti is much higher than initially thought, according to a human rights group.

stories.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
stories.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

stories.title: US trade chief lambasts WTO after failed talks
stories.summary: The US has slammed the World Trade Organization's failure to reach consensus on a key e-commerce moratorium. The top US trade official said he sees only a limited role for the international body.

stories.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
stories.summary: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for months. When the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.

stories.title: Germany news: Bavaria's state premier Markus Söder visits BMW plant in US
stories.summary: Markus Söder will view the BMW plant in Spartanburg, among other stops, after visiting Texas. Foreign Minister Wadephul is set to mark four years since the liberation of Bucha in Ukraine. DW has more.

stories.title: Long queues in Myanmar as Iran war fuel crisis deepens
stories.summary: Drivers are queuing for hours at petrol stations in Myanmar as the Iran war continues to send shockwaves across the globe.

stories.title: Inside the cockpit of RAF tanker during defensive mission against Iranian drones
stories.summary: Defence correspondent Jonathan Beale flies onboard a Royal Air Force Voyager as it refuels jets in the Middle East.

stories.title: After sex abuse claims, activists and lawmakers rethink Cesar Chavez Day
stories.summary: California has renamed Cesar Chavez Day to Farmworkers Day after the labour-rights icon was accused of sexual abuse.

stories.title: Social media firms must better enforce Australia under-16 ban, watchdog says

stories.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
stories.summary: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.

stories.title: Shock, sadness and relief in town at centre of Australia's seven-month manhunt for Dezi Freeman

stories.title: Bucha, four years on: why Ukraine cannot afford Russia's 'Peace'
stories.summary: Four years after Bucha's liberation, the illusion of diplomacy persists while the war grinds on. In this opinion piece for Euronews, Denys Glushko, Editor-in-Chief of Ukrainian outlet "Apostrophe", argues Russia's terms amount to capitulation.

stories.summary: A portál olyan képeket is közölt, amelyek szerint Fidesz alelnöke együtt is vitorlázott a tízmilliárdos tartozást felhalmozó, majd becsődölt cég vezetőjével.

stories.title: Tordai Bence visszalép. 72 óra múlva
stories.summary: A képviselő azt írta, ha Koncz Áron tiszás jelölt nem lép vissza péntek délig, akkor ő teszi ezt meg. A Tisza Párt azt közölte, hogy márpedig Koncz indulni fog a választáson.

stories.summary: Új cég válik ki a Mészáros és Mészáros Zrt.-ből, amely tulajdonos lehet majd a Ganz transzformátorokat gyártó cégében.

stories.summary: A háztartások csak az elmúlt 120 év második legenyhébb januárjában használtak fel kevesebb gázt, mint amennyit most kifizetnek majd.

stories.summary: Szerdától visszatér a tavasz, vasárnap már 20 Celsius-fok is lehet.

stories.summary: Takarókat is osztanak a betegeknek.

stories.title: Mutatjuk a tiszás politikusok oldalainál feltűnt 280 trollprofilt

stories.summary: Profibbak, mint az eddig leleplezett trollfarmok tagjai, viszont elég irracionális a viselkedésük. Hónapok óta építik a hálózatot, de csak a napokban vetették be őket, és egyelőre nem nagyon csináltak semmit. Eddig 280-at azonosítottunk.

stories.title: Repteret neveznek el Donald Trumpról Floridában
stories.summary: Ron DeSantis kormányzó aláírta a Palm Beach-i reptér nevét módosító törvényjavaslatot.

stories.title: III. Károly és Kamilla királyné április végén hivatalos látogatásra megy az Egyesült Államokba
stories.summary: II. Erzsébet 2007-es útja óta nem járt ott hivatalos állami látogatáson brit uralkodó.

stories.summary: Legalábbis az M2 Petőfi TV időközben eltávolított, a költészet napját beharangozó Facebook-posztja szerint.

stories.title: Mobilját nyomkodó 14 éves fiút ütött el a HÉV Óbudán, életveszélyes sérülésekkel vitték kórházba
stories.summary: A fiú Határ úti megállónál haladt át egy tiloson. A tanúk szerint fülhallgatót viselt, és a telefonjára figyelt.

stories.title: Újabb SpaceX-műhold robbant fel, és senki nem tudja, miért
stories.summary: A műhold darabjai a következő hetekben éghetnek el a légkörben.

stories.title: Már nem kell sokáig várni, hogy Kocsis Zoltán hagyatékából végre megnyíljon a Kocsis Archívum
stories.summary: A világhírű zongoraművész, karmester, zeneszerző hagyatéka a Ligeti György utcában találhat otthonra.

stories.title: Závecz Research: 11 százalékpontos előnye van a Tiszának a pártot választók körében
stories.summary: A teljes népességen belül 39 százalékon áll a Tisza, a Fidesz 31 százalékot szerezne.

stories.title: Meghalt Radnóti Zsuzsa dramaturg, Örkény István özvegye
stories.summary: A Vígszínház egykori dramaturgja 88 éves volt.

stories.title: Erkölcsivel
Nehéz úgy fenntartani egy demokráciát, ha azt gondoljuk, minden választás élet-halál harc
stories.summary: Ha minden választást végső ütközetként élünk meg, akkor minden eszköz megengedett lesz – a demokráciáknak viszont épp az az egyik innovációja, hogy el lehet engedni a hatalmat. Az Egyensúly Intézet vezetőivel arról beszélgettünk, hogyan újítsuk meg a demokráciát.

stories.title: Lelőttek egy fiatal antilopot Bécsben, a Schönbrunni Állatkertben
stories.summary: Az első vizsgálati eredmények szerint a lövés a kifutón kívülről érkezhetett.

stories.summary: A rurális humor Kossuth-díjas nagymestere a pár évvel ezelőtti gigászi kiállítását egy intimebbel követi. Ezek a Letűnt vidéki állatok, a disznók, kacsák, libák, bárányok és főleg: gólyák.

stories.title: Germany hosts Syria's Al-Sharaa despite human rights issues
stories.summary: Germany is keen for Syrian refugees to return home. The visit by interim Syrian President al-Sharaa highlights shifting policies on repatriation and regional stability — despite accusations of human rights violations.

stories.title: Finnish police say stray Ukrainian drone carried warhead
stories.summary: Finnish authorities are investigating two drones that crashed in southern Finland. Kyiv apologized for the incident, with Ukraine's Zelenskyy saying he and Finland's Stubb are on the same page.

stories.title: Trump says 'no problem' for Russian tanker to deliver oil to Cuba
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said Sunday that Moscow could send oil to Cuba despite Washington's de facto fuel blockade, as a Russian tanker was expected to deliver 730,000 barrels of crude to the crisis-hit island.

stories.title: Iran war: Animated map tracks Middle East strikes since February 28
stories.summary: An animated map shows drone and missile attacks across the Middle East from February 28 to March 28, including strikes attributed to Iran, Hezbollah, Israel and the United States, based on data from the NGO Acled. Orange dots represent attacks by the US and Israel, green by Iran and its allies.

stories.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
stories.summary: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

stories.title: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries are responding to rising oil prices
stories.summary: Governments around the world have introduced measures to limit the impact of price increases.

stories.title: Peace talks with Iran ongoing and progressing well, White House says
stories.summary: The comments come on the same day that President Donald Trump threatened to destroy Iran's oil export hub of Kharg Island and other oil wells and power plants if it does not agree to a deal to end the war soon.

stories.title: Russian students duped into signing contracts with military
stories.summary: University students are being recruited to serve in drone units fighting Ukraine. They are offered lucrative contracts and promised they won't be sent to the front. Reports suggest those promises are not always kept.

stories.title: Finland reports drone crashes, alleges territorial violation
stories.summary: Two unidentified drones crashed in southern Finland. According to the country's prime minister, the drones may have been Ukrainian drones that strayed into Finnish airspace.

stories.title: Urgent action needed to prevent surge in digital violence in Africa, experts say
stories.summary: A huge rise in internet users under the age of 30 has fuelled an increase in online violence against women and girls with devastating real-life effects, activists say Activists and lawyers in Africa are calling for urgent action to protect women, girls and boys as digital violence surges across the continent. A massive rise in internet users, coupled with huge numbers of people aged under 30 , has fuelled an increase in gendered online violence across the continent, according to experts, by givi

stories.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gusts An unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. The Arabian peninsula – typically dominated by arid desert climates – received up to 150mm of rain in just a few days. The deluge was caused by an abnormally strong jet stream, which helped a deep area of low pressure to

stories.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
stories.summary: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

stories.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
stories.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

stories.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
stories.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

stories.title: Germany: Stranded humpback whale moving again
stories.summary: Having barely budged the previous night, the whale stranded off Germany's Baltic coast for a week finally moved again late Monday. But after showing signs of deterioration, experts aren't sure where it's going next.

stories.title: How to fix Germany's costly health care system
stories.summary: A special commission has presented a raft of proposals aimed at curbing Germany's spiraling health care costs. But whether the government can impose them is another matter.

stories.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
stories.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

stories.title: Tech CEOs suddenly love blaming AI for mass job cuts. Why?
stories.summary: More tech leaders are pointing to job cuts caused by AI tools - and a need for more investment cash.

stories.title: Why Chinese tech companies are racing to set up in Hong Kong
stories.summary: Mainland firms are using the territory to test products and as a springboard for global expansion.

stories.title: First stop, the Moon. Next stop, Mars? Why Nasa's mission matters
stories.summary: From a race with China to lunar discoveries, the US is investing time, effort and money to head to the Moon - and beyond.

stories.title: How Australia’s seven-month-long manhunt came to an end
stories.summary: Fugitive Dezi Freeman has been wanted since he shot dead two police officers on his rural property in August last year.

stories.title: Office chair Grand Prix rolls through Japanese streets
stories.summary: The ISU-1 Grand Prix in Japan is a two-hour office chair race where teams rotate riders, entertain crowds and compete for a grand prize of 90 kilos of rice.

stories.title: Artemis II prepares to launch for the first Moon trip in 53 years
stories.summary: Commander Reid Wiseman and his crew arrived from Houston, marking their closest step yet toward the Artemis II launch.

stories.title: Netanyahu restores Latin patriarch's access to Jerusalem's Holy Sepulchre
stories.summary: The Israeli PM restored full access to the Holy Sepulchre for Latin Patriarch Pizzaballa after police barred him on Palm Sunday, sparking criticism from the EU and global leaders.

stories.title: Russia’s North Caucasus hit by massive flooding, thousands evacuated
stories.summary: Flooding triggered by heavy rain has forced thousands of people to evacuate in southern Russia’s North Caucasus region.

stories.title: 'Every housewife could be CEO of Rheinmetall': Zelenskyy hits back at German weapons boss
stories.summary: German defence company Rheinmetall issued a public statement expressing respect for Ukraine’s defence manufacturers after its CEO mocked Ukrainian weapons and drone producers over the weekend in sexist remarks.

stories.title: Spain closes its airspace to all US aircraft involved in Iran war
stories.summary: The ban comes amid strained relations between Madrid and Washington following US President Donald Trump's threat earlier this month to cut off all trade with Spain over its refusal to allow the bases to be used for Iran operations.

stories.title: Trump threatens to obliterate Iran's Kharg island oil hub if no deal reached 'shortly'
stories.summary: About 90% of Iran’s crude oil exports leave the country via the small island in the Persian Gulf's north, much of it destined for China and other Asian markets.

stories.title: What are Ukraine's new Gulf defence deals? Here is what Zelenskyy signed
stories.summary: Ukraine signed 10-year defence partnerships with three Gulf countries as Zelenskyy toured the region over the weekend. What is there for the Gulf and what will Ukraine get in return?

stories.title: France puts Freemason lodge ‘mafia’ on trial over murders and hit squads
stories.summary: Twenty-two people, including intelligence and police officers, are on trial in France over an alleged Masonic lodge assassin squad and mafia-style crimes.

stories.title: Cadbury unveils giant 55kg ‘Mega Mini Egg’ ahead of Easter
stories.summary: A 55kg Mega Mini Egg at Cadbury World wows Easter visitors, as rising cocoa costs drive chocolate prices sharply higher across the industry.

stories.title: Outrage is not enough: what actually builds peace
stories.summary: As Jews prepare for Passover – the holiday marking the exodus from Egypt – Rabbi Mendy Chitrik writes in an opinion piece for Euronews that it carries a responsibility toward the suffering of others: "So let us do good – Jews and Muslims alike."

stories.title: Inside Nasa's moon spacesuit lab ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: The Artemis II spacesuits are designed to protect the astronauts during launch and landing, as well as possible emergency scenarios.

stories.title: US embassy in Venezuela formally resumes operations after seven-year closure
stories.summary: Since the contested presidential elections in 2019, the US had run diplomatic operations for Venezuela out of neighbouring Colombia.

stories.title: Fuel shortages bring long queues to Myanmar’s capital
stories.summary: Motorists queue for hours for petrol in Myanmar’s capital, Naypyidaw, after the junta imposed fuel quotas as shortages grow.

stories.title: Germany and Syria 'working jointly' on refugee returns, Chancellor Friedrich Merz says
stories.summary: On his first trip to Germany since ousting his country's longtime strongman Bashar al-Assad in late 2024, Syria's interim leader pledged to work with Germany to enable more Syrians to return.

stories.title: France: Two new arrests over foiled Bank of America attack
stories.summary: French police arrested two more suspects over a foiled attack on Bank of America's Paris offices. The custody of the first arrested suspect arrested has been extended.

stories.title: Russia wants to legalize cars stolen in EU
stories.summary: Vehicles stolen in the EU may soon be legally registered in Russia. One German police union fears this could lead to an increase in car thefts in Europe.
